title: Poetry Perpetuates The Poet.
content:
Here I myself might likewise die,
    And utterly forgotten lie,
    But that eternal poetry
    Repullulation gives me here
    Unto the thirtieth thousand year,
    When all now dead shall reappear.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Poetry Perpetuates The Poet."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
